rows.notes: Page title at top of column. No amounts visible next to this heading; very clear handwriting and placement.
Amounts read clearly as '3 16 8' aligned with the three right-hand ruling lines. All values within valid ranges and digits are crisp.
Shillings and pence read as '14 0' to the right of the description. Handwriting of description is slightly stylized but amounts are well aligned and legible.
Amounts read as '0 1 8'. The '1' in shillings and '8' pence are clearly formed and lie correctly between the ruling lines.

Description legible as written but the numeric cells beside this row are faint / partially obscured; I cannot confidently separate digits for pounds/shillings/pence. Leaving amounts blank rather than risk column misalignment.
Second similar 'Coll. Aedis Hi' line has clearer numerals showing '0 18 0' in the three amount columns. The '18' shillings is readable though the digit shapes are slightly compressed.
Amounts to the right read as '4 12 4'. Alignment with the ruling lines is consistent; all three values are within valid ranges and clearly spaced.
Entry labelled 'Ball.' with amounts '0 12 6' visible and well aligned. The '12' shillings and '6' pence are distinct.
Numeric cluster reads '0 8 2'. Pence '2' is small but clearly formed; alignment confirms column boundaries.
Pounds and shillings read as '1 18' clearly. Pence read as '11' with a small trailing loop resembling 'ob' (half‑penny) — interpreted as 11½d. The halfpenny symbol is faint but visible.
Amounts read as '0 7 0' and aligned under shillings column. '7' shillings clear; no pence shown.
Large pounds figure '23' is clear and aligns with the pounds column. Shillings and pence read '0 0' unambiguously.

The line 'Michl.' appears with the same numeric cluster '23 0 0' directly opposite; uncertain whether this is a duplicate/seasonal label or an additional entry. The digits themselves read clearly but the row's semantic role is ambiguous.
Amounts look like '1 10 4' — the '10' shillings is slightly cramped but legible; pence '4' distinct. Description reads 'Coquo' (cook).
Amount appears as '2 0 0' (two pounds). The script for the description is slightly abbreviated but matches common ledger term for librarians/libraries.
Shillings/pence read as '6 8'; pounds '1'. Description is abbreviated (possibly 'Scil. et Quadrang.'); numerals are aligned and within valid ranges.
Amounts read '0 2 0'. The entry explicitly references chapel cleaning and the small shillings numeral is clear.
Amounts read as '0 13 4'. The '13' shillings and '4' pence are distinct and well aligned.
This supervisory entry shows '4 0 0' — the pounds figure '4' is clear. (Spelling of description is somewhat stylized but readable.)
Amounts read '1 14 8'. The '14' shillings is clear and within valid range; pence '8' legible.
Amount '0 13 4' visible next to the librarian entry. Numerals and alignment are clear.
Read as '1 3 0'. The pounds '1' and shillings '3' are distinct; description reads 'Bedell of Beggars' (bedel).
Amount '1 0 0' appears clearly opposite this entry.

rows.description: S. Newington Vic. ad Test. Annuum.
rows.notes: Line reads '10 0 0' distinctly. The clerical abbreviation for the vicar is legible; numeric values are clear.
A second 'Michl.' entry appears with '10 0 0' opposite it. The numerals are clear but the role of this line (label vs separate entry) is ambiguous; recorded as entry because amounts are present.
Reads '5 15 0' (five pounds fifteen shillings). The '15' is clearly formed and lies inside the shillings column.
Amount recorded as '5 8 0'. Clear digits, alignment consistent with other rows.
The numeric area opposite this label appears to show zeros (no charge), but the digits are faint. I record as '0 0 0' with moderate confidence.
Amounts read as '0 8 1' — the '8' shillings and '1' pence are legible and aligned. No fractional pence.
Recorded as '0 11 0' (eleven shillings). Numerals are clear and within expected ranges.

Bottom total reads '105 15 0' clearly for pounds/shillings/pence, but there is a small faint mark next to the pence that could be a half‑penny symbol. I record pence_whole as 0 and include a half‑penny fraction (0.5) with moderate confidence due to the faintness of the fractional mark.
